title: Understand: ecosystem dynamics
description: Check your understanding of ecosystem dynamics in this set of free, standards-aligned practice questions.

assessment_items.question_content:
**What does it mean that ecosystems are dynamic?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Their characteristics vary over time.
Their characteristics remain the same over time.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Choose the best word or phrase to fill in the blank.**

>Ecosystems are dynamic. This means that ecosystems’ characteristics \_\_\_\_\_\_ over time.

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: vary
remain the same

assessment_items.question_content:
**Why are ecosystems considered to be dynamic?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: because their characteristics vary over time
because their characteristics remain the same over time

assessment_items.question_content:
**What is the most likely result of a disruption to one part of an ecosystem?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: changes to many of the ecosystem’s populations
a change in one of the ecosystem’s populations

assessment_items.question_content:
**What does a disruption in one part of an ecosystem typically lead to?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: a change in many populations
a change in a single population

assessment_items.question_content:
**Choose the best word or phrase to fill in the blank.**

>A disruption to one part of an ecosystem typically leads to a change in \_\_\_\_\_\_ of the ecosystem’s populations.

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: many
only one

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of these describes a change in a *nonliving* (abiotic) part of an ecosystem?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: a decrease in the amount of snow that falls
a decrease in a predator population size
a decrease in the number of species in the ecosystem

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of these describes a change in a *living* (biotic) part of an ecosystem?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the addition of a new species to the area
a long term increase in temperature
an increase in the amount of rain that falls

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of these describes a change in a *living* (biotic) part of an ecosystem?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: an increase in a predator population size
an increase in the number of hot days per year
a decrease in the amount of rain that falls

assessment_items.question_content:
**A certain ecosystem change makes it less likely that organisms in a population will survive and reproduce. How will this change likely affect the population?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The population will get smaller.
The population will get larger.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Choose the best word to fill in the blank.**

>Some ecosystem changes make it less likely that organisms in a population will survive and reproduce. These types of changes can cause a population to get \_\_\_\_\_\_.

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: smaller
larger

assessment_items.question_content:
**Choose the best word to fill in the blank.**

>Some ecosystem changes make it more likely that organisms in a population will survive and reproduce. These types of changes can cause a population to get \_\_\_\_\_\_.

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: larger
smaller
